volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Trinummus (Section 2.4.537b - Section 3.1.607b)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Trinummus: The Three Pieces of Money

sections.label: Section 2.4.537b
sections.translation: Preserve me from this piece of land .

sections.label: Section 2.4.538
sections.original: Magis apage dicas , si omnia ex me audiveris .
sections.translation: Preserve me from it , you would say still more , if you were to hear everything from me . For there every other tree has been blasted with lightning ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.540
sections.translation: the hogs die [*] there most shockingly of inflammation in the throat ; the sheep are scabby , as bare of all wool , see , as is this hand of mine . And then , besides , there is not one of the Syrian natives [*] , a race which is the most hardy of men , who could exist there for six months ; so surely do all die there of the solstitial fever [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.4.545
sections.original: Credo ego istuc , Stasime , ita esse ; sed Campans genus
sections.translation: I believe , Stasimus [*] , that it is so ; but the Campanian [*] race much outdoes that of the Syrians in hardiness . But , really , that piece of land , as I have heard you describe it , is one to which it were proper for all wicked men to be sent for the public good . Just as they tell of the Islands of the Blest ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.550
sections.original: quo cuncti qui aetatem egerint caste suam
sections.translation: where all meet together who have passed their lives uprightly : on the other hand , it seems proper that all evildoers should be packed off there , since it is a place of such a character .

sections.label: Section 2.4.553
sections.original: Hospitium est calamitatis : quid verbis opust ?
sections.translation: ’ Tis a very receptacle of calamity . What need is there of many words ? Look for any bad thing whatsoever , there you may find it .

sections.label: Section 2.4.555
sections.original: At tu hercle et illi et alibi .
sections.translation: But , i ’ faith , you may find it there and elsewhere too .

sections.label: Section 2.4.555b
sections.translation: Please , take care not to say that I told you of this .

sections.label: Section 2.4.556b
sections.translation: You have told it me in perfect secrecy .

sections.label: Section 2.4.557
sections.original: Quin hic quidem cúpit illum ab se abalienarier ,
sections.translation: For he , indeed ( pointing at LESBONICUS ) , wishes it to be got rid of from himself , if he can find any one to impose upon [*] about it .

sections.label: Section 2.4.559
sections.translation: I ’ faith , this land shall never become my property .

sections.label: Section 2.4.559b
sections.translation: Aye , if you keep in your senses .

sections.label: Section 2.4.560
sections.original: lepide hercle dé agro ego hunc senem deterrui ;
sections.translation: ( Aside .) I ’ faith , I have cleverly frightened [*] the old fellow away from this land ; for , if my master had parted with it , there is nothing for us to live upon .

sections.label: Section 2.4.562
sections.translation: Lesbonicus , I now return to you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.562b
sections.translation: Tell me , if you please , what has he been saying to you ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.563b
sections.translation: What do you suppose ? He is a man [*] ; he wishes to become a free man , but he has not the money to give .

sections.label: Section 2.4.565
sections.original: Et ego esse locuples , verum nequiquam volo .
sections.translation: And I wish to be rich , but all in vain .

sections.label: Section 2.4.566
sections.original: Licitumst , si velles ; nunc , quom nihil est , non licet .
sections.translation: You might have been , if you had chosen ; now , since you have nothing , you cannot be .

sections.label: Section 2.4.567
sections.translation: What are you talking about to yourself , Stasimus ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.567b
sections.translation: About that which you were saying just now : if you had chosen formerly , you might have been rich ; now you are wishing too late .

sections.label: Section 2.4.569
sections.original: De dote mecum convenire nil potest :
sections.translation: No terms can be come to with me about the marriage-portion ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.570
sections.original: quid tibi lubet tute agito cum nato meo .
sections.translation: whatever pleases you , do you transact it yourself with my son . Now , I ask for your sister for my son ; and may the matter turn out well . What now ? are you still considering ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.573
sections.original: Quid istíc ? quando ita vis : di bene vortant . spondeo .
sections.translation: What — about that matter ? Since you will have it so — may the Gods prosper it — I promise her .

sections.label: Section 2.4.574
sections.original: Numquam edepol quoiquam tam expectatus filius
sections.translation: Never , by my troth , was a son

sections.label: Section 2.4.575
sections.original: natus quam illuc est spondeo natum mihi .
sections.translation: born so ardently longed for by any one , as was that expression I promise her , when born for me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.576
sections.translation: The Gods will prosper all your plans .

sections.label: Section 2.4.576b
sections.translation: So I wish .

sections.label: Section 2.4.577
sections.original: Sed , Stasime , abi huc ad meam sororem ad Calliclem ,
sections.translation: Now , Stasimus , go you there ( points to the house which he has sold to CALLICLES ) to the house of Callicles , to my sister ; tell her how this matter has been arranged .

sections.label: Section 2.4.578b
sections.translation: I will go .

sections.label: Section 2.4.579
sections.translation: And congratulate my sister .

sections.label: Section 2.4.579b
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 2.4.580
sections.original: I hac , Lesbonice , mecum , ut coram nuptiis
sections.translation: Come this way with me , Lesbonicus , that a day may be agreed on for the nuptials , in the presence of Lysiteles : this agreement we will ratify on that same day .

sections.label: Section 2.4.582
sections.original: Tu ístuc cura quod iussi . ego iam hic ero .—
sections.translation: Do you take care of that which I have asked you . I shall be here directly . ( Exit LESBONICUS .)

sections.label: Section 2.4.583
sections.translation: Tell Callicles to meet me —

sections.label: Section 2.4.583b
sections.translation: But rather do you go now —

sections.label: Section 2.4.584
sections.original: De dote ut videat quid opus sit facto .
sections.translation: That he may see what is necessary to be done about the portion .

sections.label: Section 2.4.584b
sections.translation: Do go now .

sections.label: Section 2.4.585
sections.original: Nam certumst sine dote haud dare .
sections.translation: For I have determined not to give her without a portion .

sections.label: Section 2.4.585b
sections.translation: But rather do you go now .

sections.label: Section 2.4.586
sections.original: Neque enim illi damno umquam esse patiar
sections.translation: And I will never allow it to be a detriment to her by reason of —

sections.label: Section 2.4.586b
sections.translation: Do be off now .

sections.label: Section 2.4.587
sections.translation: My recklessness —

sections.label: Section 2.4.587b
sections.translation: Do go now [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.4.587c
sections.translation: It seems by no means just , but that , since I have done wrong —

sections.label: Section 2.4.588b
sections.translation: Do go now .

sections.label: Section 2.4.589
sections.translation: It should be chiefly a detriment to myself .

sections.label: Section 2.4.589b
sections.translation: Do go now .

sections.label: Section 2.4.589c
sections.translation: O my father !

sections.label: Section 2.4.590
sections.translation: and shall I ever see you again ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.590b
sections.original: I modo , i modo , i modo .
sections.translation: Do go now . Go — go now .

sections.label: Section 2.4.591
sections.original: tandem impetravi abiret . di vestram fidem ,
sections.translation: At length I have prevailed on him to go . In the name of the immortal Gods , i ’ faith , ’ tis a matter well managed by wrongful means of performance , inasmuch as our piece of land is safe ; although even now ’ tis still a very doubtful matter what may be the result of this affair .

sections.label: Section 2.4.595
sections.original: sed id si alienatur , actumst de collo meo ,
sections.translation: But , if the land is parted with , ’ tis all over [*] with my neck ; I must carry a buckler in foreign lands , a helmet too , and my baggage . He will be running away from the city when the nuptials have been celebrated ; he will be going hence to extreme and utter ruin , somewhere or other , to serve as a soldier , either to Asia or to Cilicia [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.4.600
sections.original: ibo huc quo mi imperatumst , etsi odi hanc domum ,
sections.translation: I will go there ( looking at the door of the house bouight by CALLICLES ) , where he has ordered me to go , although I detest this house ever since he has driven us out of our abode .

sections.label: Section 3.1.602
sections.original: Quó modo tu istuc , Stasime , dixti ?
sections.translation: To what effect were you speaking about this , Stasimus ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.602b
sections.translation: That Lesbonicus , the son of my master , has betrothed his sister ; in those terms .

sections.label: Section 3.1.604
sections.translation: To what person has he betrothed her ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.604b
sections.translation: To Lysiteles , the son of Philto ; without a portion , too .

sections.label: Section 3.1.605b
sections.original: Sine dote ille illam in tantas divitias dabit ?
sections.translation: Without a portion , will he marry her into a family so rich [*] ? You are telling me a thing not to be credited .

sections.label: Section 3.1.606b
sections.translation: Why , faith , you would be for never believing . If you don ’ t believe this , at all events I shall be believing —

sections.label: Section 3.1.607b
sections.translation: What ?
